name: Snowflake Arctic Embed
tagline: Snowflake's open family of retrieval-tuned text embedding models
seoTitle: Snowflake Arctic Embed - Open Text Embedding Models
metaDescription: Snowflake Arctic Embed is an open family of text embedding models tuned for search and RAG, with multilingual v2.0 variants and Apache-2.0 licensing.
overview: Snowflake Arctic Embed is a family of open-source text embedding models built for retrieval. The models turn queries and documents into vectors so you can rank documents by relevance, which is the core building block of semantic search and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines.

The suite comes in several sizes, from a 22M-parameter xs model with 384 dimensions up to the 335M-parameter l model with 1024 dimensions, plus newer multilingual v2.0 variants. This range lets you trade off accuracy against latency and cost: the smaller models fit tight budgets, while the largest can stand in for closed-source embedding APIs.

It fits in the embedding-models category alongside other retrieval encoders. The models load through standard tooling such as Sentence Transformers, Hugging Face Transformers, and Transformers.js, so they drop into existing search and RAG stacks without a custom runtime.
features: Multiple model sizes (xs, s, m, m-long, l) covering 22M to 335M parameters and 384 to 1024 embedding dimensions
Tuned for retrieval quality on the MTEB/BEIR benchmarks at each size profile
Multilingual v2.0 variants (m-v2.0 and l-v2.0) for cross-language retrieval
Loadable via Sentence Transformers, Hugging Face Transformers, and Transformers.js (JavaScript)
Uses a query prefix and CLS-token pooling to improve search relevance
Apache-2.0 licensed and published on Hugging Face for open use

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest path is the Sentence Transformers library, which loads a model by its Hugging Face ID and encodes text with a single call.

steps.heading: Install Sentence Transformers
steps.body: Install the library that wraps model loading and encoding.

steps.heading: Embed queries and documents and rank by similarity
steps.body: Load a model, encode queries with the query prompt and documents without it, then score by dot product.
steps.code:
import torch
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
from torch.nn.functional import normalize

# Model constant.
MODEL_ID = "Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5"

# Your queries and docs.
queries = ['what is snowflake?', 'Where can I get the best tacos?']
documents = ['The Data Cloud!', 'Mexico City of Course!']

# Load the model.
model = SentenceTransformer(
    MODEL_ID, model_kwargs=dict(add_pooling_layer=False),
)

# Generate text embeddings.
query_embeddings = model.encode(queries, prompt_name="query")
document_embeddings = model.encode(documents)

# Scores via dotproduct.
scores = query_embeddings @ document_embeddings.T

# Pretty-print the results.
for query, query_scores in zip(queries, scores):
    doc_score_pairs = list(zip(documents, query_scores))
    doc_score_pairs = sorted(doc_score_pairs, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    print(f'Query: "{query}"')
    for document, score in doc_score_pairs:
        print(f'Score: {score:.4f} | Document: "{document}"')
    print()

useCases: Power semantic search over a document collection by ranking documents against a user query
Build the retrieval step of a RAG pipeline that feeds relevant context to an LLM
Run cross-language retrieval with the multilingual v2.0 models
Replace a closed-source embedding API with a self-hosted, Apache-2.0 model to control cost and data
